items.title: MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002)
items.why_it_matters: Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language/amounts and wraparound support intent through the FY27 budget process.

items.title: U.S. Department of Labor RSS: releases.xml
items.why_it_matters: Federal workforce-development grants and policy signals frequently originate at DOL/ETA—high relevance for community colleges.

items.title: Will There Be a Second Compact?
items.summary: Will There Be a Second Compact? sara.custer@in… Thu, 03/05/2026 - 03:00 AM The Department of Education will struggle to find broad acceptance of a new iteration of the Compact for Academic Excellence in Higher Education without true partnership with the sector. Byline(s) Sara Custer

items.title: How RFK Jr. Is Trying to Revamp Medical School
items.summary: The health secretary, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., has tapped into an old debate about how much doctors should know about nutrition. But some of his ideas, and tactics, concern medical experts.

items.title: After Pete Hegseth Cut Pentagon Funding, Harvard Offers Options for Military
items.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has said the Pentagon would end funds supporting active-duty service members at Harvard. The school is offering military students alternatives to defer or go elsewhere.

items.title: Hegseth Cuts Ties With Yale, Georgetown and Other Top Universities
items.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth accused Yale, Princeton, Brown and other elite colleges and think tanks of indoctrinating service members with liberal ideologies.

items.title: Columbia Student Detained by ICE Promotes ‘Beauty’ and ‘Brains’ Online
items.summary: Elmina Aghayeva has 114,000 followers on Instagram and has seemingly never posted about politics, unlike other Columbia University students detained by immigration officers.

items.title: Racist, Antisemitic Slurs Surface in Florida GOP Group Chat
items.summary: After the secretary of the Miami-Dade County Republican Party created the chat for college students, it devolved into slurs against Black and Jewish people.

items.title: U.S. Schools Are Betting Big on A.I. Will New York City Be Next?
items.summary: This could be a pivotal year in shaping what role artificial intelligence plays in American schools. Some families want Mayor Mamdani to hit the brakes.

items.title: Columbia Is Investigated for Handling of Sex-Abuse Claims Against Doctor
items.summary: Attorney General Letitia James is investigating the university for its actions after the 2012 arrest of Robert Hadden, a former Columbia gynecologist convicted of sex crimes.

items.title: Judge Vacates Punishments of Columbia Students Who Occupied a Building
items.summary: Pro-Palestinian demonstrators who had taken over Hamilton Hall in 2024 faced expulsions, suspensions and the revocation of their degrees.

Critics say her work is creating barriers to college access. Byline(s) Jessica Blake Katherine Knott

linkedin_drafts.title: MA budget watch: SUCCESS Fund signals
linkedin_drafts.text:
Massachusetts’ Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002) is one of the most concrete examples of policy translating into student-support capacity—peer mentors, skills workshops, transfer-related activities, and targeted advising.

Anchor link I check during budget season: MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002) (https://budget.digital.mass.gov/govbudget/fy27/appropriations/education/higher-education/71004002/)

Question for colleagues: which SUCCESS-funded intervention has produced the clearest retention/completion gains on your campus?

#CommunityColleges #StudentSuccess #AcademicAdvising #HigherEdPolicy #Massachusetts

linkedin_drafts.title: Federal policy watch: what community colleges can act on
linkedin_drafts.text:
Federal policy and funding signals matter most when they change what colleges can *do* this semester: aid eligibility, workforce grants, reporting requirements, or program approval.

One item on my radar: U.S. Department of Labor RSS: releases.xml (https://www.dol.gov/rss/releases.xml)

If you’re in advising or student success, what’s the most helpful way you’ve communicated major policy shifts to students—FAQs, short videos, office hours, or targeted messaging?

#HigherEdPolicy #CommunityColleges #FinancialAid #StudentSuccess #AcademicAdvising

linkedin_drafts.title: AI in higher ed: governance beats hype
linkedin_drafts.text:
AI adoption in higher education is increasingly a governance challenge: approved use cases, human oversight, privacy/procurement, and how we measure impact.

One item I’m tracking: U.S. Schools Are Betting Big on A.I. Will New York City Be Next? (https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/02/nyregion/nyc-ai-schools-mamdani.html)

For student support/advising: what guardrails have worked well at your institution?

#AIinEducation #HigherEdLeadership #AcademicAdvising #StudentSuccess #EdTech
